texto: To my mind's eye, my buried memories of Brandham Hall are like effects of chiaroscuro, patches of light and dark: it is only with effort that I see them in terms of colour. There are things I know, though I don't know how I know them, and things that I remember. Certain things are established in my mind as facts, but no picture attaches to them; on the other hand there are pictures unverified by any fact which recur obsessively, like the landscape of a dream.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a L.P. Hartley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “To my mind's eye, my buried memories of Brandham Hall are like effects of chiaroscuro, patches of…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, memory, things pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao-noturna e reflexao.

texto: Detective-story writers give this thrill by exploiting the resources of the possible; however improbable the happenings in a detective story, they can and must be explained in terms that satisfy the reason. But in a ghost story, where natural laws are dispensed with, the whole point is that the happenings cannot be so explained. A ghost story that is capable of a rational explanation is as much an anomaly as a detective story that isn’t. The one is in revolt against a materialistic conception of the universe, whereas the other depends on it.
explicacao: Orientação direta de L.P. Hartley sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Detective-story writers give this thrill by exploiting the resources of the possible; however imp…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, ambiguity, ghost-story, útil em momentos de termino e ghost-story.

texto: I had never met a lord before, nor had I ever expected to meet one. It didn't matter what he looked like: he was a lord first, and a human being, with a face and limbs and body, long, long after.

texto: If personality expresses itself by acts of discrimination, and discrimination, besides being taboo, has no material to work on, what becomes of personality? It shrinks, it atrophies, it dies.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de L.P. Hartley que provoca temas ligados a trabalho. Ao questionar “If personality expresses itself by acts of discrimination, and discrimination, besides being tabo…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre trabalho, comparison, discrimination, com foco em trabalho e discrimination.

texto: Even the most impassioned devotee of the ghost story would admit that the taste for it is slightly abnormal, a survival, perhaps, from adolescence, a disease of deficiency suffered by those whose lives and imaginations do not react satisfactorily to normal experience and require an extra thrill

texto: I was aware of something stable in his nature. Ha game me a feeling of security, as if nothing I said or did would change his opinion of me. I never found his pleasantries irksome, partly, no doubt, because he was a Viscount, but, partly, too, because I respected his self-discipline. He had very little to laugh about, I thought, and yet he laughed. His gaiety had a background of the hospital and the battlefield. I felt he had some inner reserve of strength which no reverse, however serious, would break down.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a L.P. Hartley: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I was aware of something stable in his nature. Ha game me a feeling of security, as if nothing I …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, spirit, strength pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e strength.
